title: Use-After-Free, Double Free, and Stale Aliases · Lifetime Workshop
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working generation-checked handle repair while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of generation-checked handle repair while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace allocation, publication, last valid use, deallocation, stale use, and address reuse to diagnose use-after-free.
Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.
Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Uaf Causal Trace
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 2396. Produce bounded C evidence to reject stale handles with a generation check; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

hints: For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Quarantine Generation Defense, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Quarantine Generation Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Quarantine Generation Defense, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 3906. Produce bounded C evidence to reject stale handles with a generation check; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use generation counters, handles, quarantine, or delayed reclamation where stale asynchronous references cannot be excluded.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Quarantine Generation Defense, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: Address uniqueness across time is a dependable object identity.

options.text: Keep the plausible Quarantine Generation Defense output and hide the violated generation-checked handle repair assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Quarantine Generation Defense.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Quarantine Generation Defense case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe. This does not satisfy Quarantine Generation Defense for generation-checked handle repair.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Quarantine Generation Defense condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Quarantine Generation Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Quarantine Generation Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 9859. Produce bounded C evidence to reject stale handles with a generation check; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use generation counters, handles, quarantine, or delayed reclamation where stale asynchronous references cannot be excluded.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Quarantine Generation Defense.
steps.why: Retrieving Quarantine Generation Defense through the concrete generation-checked handle repair case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Quarantine Generation Defense, keep Quarantine Generation Defense connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use generation counters, handles, quarantine, or delayed reclamation where stale asynchronous references cannot be excluded.
